title: Single DIY Entryway Drawer Bench
summary: A compact plywood entryway bench with one full-width storage drawer, an inset footer for boots, and a top that overhangs the sides by 1/4".
description:
This bench is designed to nest efficiently on a sheet of plywood — if you're building three at once, cutting them as a set uses only two sheets instead of three, so lay out all the parts before cutting if you're making more than one. Every panel joint here is nailed and glued edge-to-face, not pocket-holed, so keep the reference overhangs exact: the top overhangs the sides by 1/4" on both ends, and that's what gives the bench its finished look rather than a boxy one.

Build and install the drawer without its face first, centering it in the opening with an even 1/8" gap, then attach the face from inside the drawer box (leaning the drawer toward you to reach the back of the face) — nailing from inside hides most of the fasteners on the finished front.

cutList.note: Overhangs the sides by 1/4" on each side.
Two boards edge-joined to make the full face width.

steps.title: Cut the parts
steps.body: Save your scraps — laying out the cuts efficiently means three benches only need two sheets of plywood.

steps.title: Attach the sides, bottom, and top
steps.body: Use 2" nails and glue to nail through the sides into the end edges of the bottom, following the diagram measurements. Then nail the top to the sides with 2" nails and glue — the top overhangs the sides by 1/4" on both sides.

steps.title: Add the inset footer
steps.body: Nail the inset footer in place, keeping the outside edges flush, using 2" nails and glue through the sides and bottom into its edges.

steps.title: Add the trim
steps.body: Nail the top front trim to the top and sides of the bench with 2" nails and glue. Nail the side trim in place, nailing into the side edges, the bottom edge, and the footer (use 1-1/4" nails into the footer). Finish the trim by nailing the bottom trim to the bench, using 2" nails and glue into the top, and 1-1/4" nails into the footer.

steps.title: Build the drawer box
steps.body: Use 2" nails and glue to nail through the drawer bottom into the edges of the drawer sides.

steps.title: Add the drawer front and back
steps.body: Place the front and back boards in the drawer and nail through the bottom and sides into their edges with 2" nails.

steps.title: Install the drawer
steps.body: Attach the drawer slides to the bench and drawer box, then insert and push the drawer into the bench without its face.

steps.title: Attach the drawer face
steps.body: Position the drawer face on the drawer with an even 1/8" gap all around. Glue and nail one nail per corner with 1-1/4" nails, then pull the drawer out, lean it toward you, and nail into the back of the face from the inside to hide most of the nail holes. Avoid nailing where the handle will go.

images.alt: A compact plywood entryway bench with a single storage drawer
